items.title: investingLive Americas FX news wrap 1 Jul: Central bankers worry about inflation
items.summary: US stocks close lower. Midday rise in the Nasdaq fizzles Crude oil futures settle at $68.58.Lowest settle going back to the start of the Iran War. USDMCA: US did not agree to renew USMCA in current form. U.S.-Iran talks focus on Hormuz as toll dispute threatens nuclear deal Atlanta Fed GDPNow tumbles to 1.2% from 2.5% last. Fox Reporting: Trump decided not to renew USMCA ECB's Kassik: One more rate hike remains a reasonable expectation EIA weekly crude oil inventories -3.775 million versus -4.466 million estimate US construction spending for May 0.1% versus 0.1% expected US ISM Manufacturing PMI for June 53.3 versus 54.0 estimate US S&P global manufacturing PMI final for June 53.9 vs 55.7 prior ECB Forum: Lagarde had perfect monetary policy outlook to raise rates. Meta says they are building a cloud business to sell excess AI compute OPEC+ Is likely to raise oil output quotas for August by 188K BPD. US June ADP employment data +98K vs +118K expected investingLive European markets wrap: Oil keeps lower, tech shares retreat on July turn Morning Kickstart: Markets brace for a busy day. What are the technicals telling traders? Central bankers from the Federal Reserve, European Central Bank, Bank of England and Bank of Canada gathered on the same stage at the ECB Forum on Central Banking in Sintra. The central bank heads delivered a broadly cautious message, emphasizing that while inflation risks have eased, the fight to secure price stability is not over. Officials also spent considerable time discussing the challenges of central bank communication, the uncertain impact of artificial intelligence, and financial stability risks. Price stability remains the common objective. ECB President Christine Lagarde, Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, BOE Governor Andrew Bailey, and BOC Governor Tiff Macklem all stressed that inflation remains their primary focus, even as inflation pressures have moderated in recent months. Forward guidance is falling out of favor. Lagarde said one of her biggest regrets was being constrained by forward guidance, while Bailey warned that virtually anything central bankers say can be interpreted as policy guidance. Warsh echoed those concerns, saying if communication becomes an obstacle to good policymaking, it should be changed/eliminated, adding that the Fed will move away from forward guidance. Despite being pressed, by the moderator, there was an equal fight back against giving a bias. Fed's Warsh signals a reset. Warsh repeatedly emphasized returning to "first principles," announcing the creation of Fed task forces to reassess policymaking. He said interest rates should remain the Fed's primary policy tool, reaffirmed the central bank's independence, and stressed the commitment to returning inflation to the 2% target while fulfilling the Fed's dual mandate. AI viewed as a long-term opportunity—but with uncertainty. Warsh expressed the strongest optimism, arguing the U.S. is likely to be a major beneficiary of the AI revolution, supporting stronger productivity, employment, and prosperity. Bailey was more cautious, saying the impact of AI on employment remains uncertain and requires closer international cooperation and oversight. Economic conditions differ across regions. Lagarde said euro area inflation risks are now more balanced and rejected concerns about stagflation. Bailey pointed to a softening U.K. economy and labor market but said rate cuts are not currently under consideration. Macklem described Canada's economy as soft while inflation remains above target, justifying the Bank of Canada's decision to leave rates unchanged. Financial stability concerns remain elevated. Macklem warned that elevated equity valuations and growing hedge fund participation in sovereign debt markets could amplify risks if repo market disruptions trigger forced deleveraging. Bailey also highlighted the need to monitor potential tail risks that could disrupt financial markets. Bottom line Despite different domestic economic conditions, the discussion revealed broad agreement among policymakers. Inflation has improved but has not been fully defeated, policy decisions will remain data dependent, and central banks are becoming increasingly reluctant to rely on explicit forward guidance. At the same time, AI is emerging as a key long-term theme—with optimism about its economic potential tempered by uncertainty over its impact on employment, inflation, and financial stability. ISM comes in weaker than expectations The June ISM Manufacturing PMI came in at 53.3, slightly below the 54.0 consensus estimate and down from 54.0 in May, but the report still pointed to a manufacturing sector that remains on solid footing. June marked the sixth consecutive month that the index has remained above the 50.0 expansion threshold, following a 10-month period of contraction. The reading is also consistent with continued growth in the broader U.S. economy, with ISM noting that a PMI above 47.5 has historically signaled overall economic expansion. Looking beneath the headline, the report showed some moderation in activity after May's strong performance. New orders eased to 56.0 from 56.8, while production slowed to 52.2 from 54.3, indicating demand and output remain healthy but are expanding at a slower pace. Employment improved to 49.7 from 48.6, though it remained below the 50.0 level, signaling manufacturing payrolls continued to contract. New export orders slipped back into contraction at 48.5, highlighting softer overseas demand. One of the more encouraging aspects of the report was on the inflation front. The Prices Paid index fell sharply to 73.0 from 82.1, well below the 78.0 consensus estimate, suggesting that input cost pressures eased meaningfully during the month. Meanwhile, supplier deliveries slowed less dramatically than in May, pointing to continued improvement in supply-chain conditions, while inventories remained in contraction as manufacturers kept stock levels lean. Overall, the June ISM report suggested that U.S. manufacturing remains in expansion mode but has shifted into a more measured pace following May's surge. The combination of continued growth in new orders and production, moderating price pressures, and a slight improvement in employment should be viewed as constructive. While the slowdown in export demand bears watching, the report remains consistent with an economy that continues to grow at a moderate pace without showing signs of overheating. U.S. construction spending was little changed in May, rising 0.1% to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of $2.210 trillion after an upwardly revised April reading, but remained 1.5% below its level from a year earlier. Through the first five months of 2026, construction spending totaled $858.4 billion, down 2.7% from the same period in 2025, highlighting the sector's softer trend. Private construction was essentially flat, as a 0.3% increase in residential spending was offset by a 0.3% decline in nonresidential projects. Public construction provided the brightest spot, rising 0.5% on the month, led by 0.6% gains in both educational and highway construction. Overall, the report points to a construction sector that is stabilizing on a month-to-month basis but continues to face headwinds compared with a year ago. The USD ends the day higher. The U.S. dollar finished the session broadly stronger against the major currencies, posting gains against six of the seven most actively traded counterparts. The lone exception was the British pound, which managed to edge higher against the greenback. Among the biggest movers, the Australian dollar fell 0.36% to 0.6894, while the euro was the weakest, declining 0.38% to 1.1378. The New Zealand dollar slipped 0.11% to 0.5670. The CHF and the CAD fell by 0.15% and 0.13% respectively. Against the Japanese yen, USDJPY edged up 0.01% to 162.56, reflecting little net change on the day. However, the USDJPY did reach a new 40 year high of 162.83 before moving lower in the US session The only currency to outperform the dollar was the British pound, with GBPUSD rising 0.13% to 1.3277, making sterling the strongest major currency versus the greenback. Overall, the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) gained 0.20% to 101.388, underscoring the dollar's broad-based strength into the close. US stocks are ending the day down with the Dow near unchanged, the S&P down -0.22% and the Nasdaq falling -0.66%. Meta announced early in the day that they would look to sell their excess AI capacity which helped Meta (is it a new business flow) and hurt some of the chip. AI infrastructure companies. The word excess supply is scary to a market that is being built on scarcity, not excess. Meta rose 8.81% Micron fell -10.57% and is now below the closing level from before the earnings release last week at $1048.50. The price close that $1032.28. Intel fell -9.03%. AMD fell -6.29% Sandisk fell -10.62% Broadcom fell 2.23% Nebius fell -17.01% in the US that market, yields moved higher with a steeper yield curve: 2 year yield 4.178%, +3.9 basis points. 5 year yield 4.238%, +5.08 basis points. 10 year yield 4.481%, +6.0 basis points 30 year yield 4.975%, +7.2 basis points IN other markets: Crude oil is closing lower with the price trading down -1.41% at $68.09 Gold is trading up $28 or 0.69% at $4035 Silver is trading up $0.53 or 0.97% at $59.07 Bitcoin is trading just below $60,000 at $59938. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 美股收低。纳斯达克指数午盘涨幅未能维持。原油期货收于68.58美元，创伊朗战争爆发以来最低收盘价。USDMCA：美国未同意以现有形式续签《美墨加协定》（USMCA）。美伊谈判聚焦霍尔木兹海峡，而过路费争端威胁着核协议。亚特兰大联储GDPNow预测值从上次的2.5%骤降至1.2%。福克斯报道：特朗普决定不续签《美墨加协定》（USMCA） 欧洲央行卡西克表示： 再加息一次仍是合理预期。美国能源信息署（EIA）每周原油库存为-377.5万桶，低于预期的-446.6万桶。美国5月建筑支出为0.1%，与预期持平。美国6月ISM制造业PMI为53.3，低于预期的54.0。美国标普全球6月制造业PMI终值为53.9，低于前值55.7。欧洲央行论坛： 拉加德对加息的货币政策展望堪称完美。Meta表示正在构建云业务以出售多余的AI算力。OPEC+可能将8月份的原油产量配额上调18.8万桶/日。美国6月ADP就业数据为+9.8万，低于预期值+11.8万。investingLive欧洲市场综述：油价持续走低，科技股因7月转折点而回落。晨间快讯：市场为忙碌的一天做好准备。 技术指标向交易员传递了什么信号？来自美联储、欧洲央行、英格兰银行和加拿大央行的央行行长们齐聚辛特拉举行的欧洲央行中央银行论坛，同台亮相。这些央行行长们传递了总体审慎的信息，强调尽管通胀风险已有所缓解，但维护物价稳定的斗争尚未结束。官员们还花了不少时间讨论了央行沟通面临的挑战、人工智能带来的不确定影响以及金融稳定风险。价格稳定仍是共同目标。欧洲央行行长克里斯蒂娜·拉加德、美联储主席凯文·沃什、英格兰银行行长安德鲁·贝利以及加拿大央行行长蒂夫·麦克莱姆均强调，尽管近几个月通胀压力有所缓解，但通胀仍是他们的首要关注点。前瞻性指引正逐渐失宠。拉加德表示，她最大的遗憾之一就是受到前瞻性指引的束缚，而贝利则警告称，央行行长们说的几乎任何话都可能被解读为政策指引。 沃什对此表示认同，称如果沟通成为良好政策制定的障碍，就应加以改变或消除，并补充说美联储将放弃前瞻性指引。尽管主持人不断追问，但他同样坚决反对透露任何政策倾向。美联储的沃什释放出政策重置的信号。沃什多次强调回归“第一性原理”，并宣布成立美联储特别工作组以重新评估政策制定。他表示，利率应继续作为美联储的主要政策工具，重申了央行的独立性，并强调了在履行美联储双重使命的同时，将通胀率回归至2%目标的承诺。人工智能被视为长期机遇——但存在不确定性。沃什表达了最强烈的乐观态度，认为美国很可能成为人工智能革命的主要受益者，这将推动生产率、就业和繁荣的提升。贝利则更为谨慎，他表示人工智能对就业的影响仍存在不确定性，需要更紧密的国际合作和监管。各地区的经济状况各不相同。拉加德表示，欧元区通胀风险目前更为均衡，并驳斥了关于滞胀的担忧。 贝利指出英国经济和劳动力市场正在走软，但表示目前尚未考虑降息。麦克莱姆将加拿大经济描述为疲软，同时通胀率仍高于目标水平，这为加拿大央行维持利率不变的决定提供了依据。金融稳定方面的担忧依然居高不下。麦克莱姆警告称，若回购市场动荡引发强制去杠杆，过高的股票估值以及对冲基金在主权债务市场参与度的提升可能会放大风险。贝利也强调，有必要密切关注可能扰乱金融市场的潜在尾部风险。核心结论 尽管各国国内经济状况各异，但讨论显示政策制定者之间存在广泛共识。通胀形势虽有改善但尚未完全得到遏制，政策决策仍将取决于数据，且各国央行越来越不愿依赖明确的前瞻性指引。与此同时，人工智能正成为一个关键的长期主题——对其经济潜力的乐观预期，正因其对就业、通胀和金融稳定的影响存在不确定性而受到抑制。 ISM数据表现弱于预期6月ISM制造业PMI为53.3，略低于54.0的共识预期，且较5月的54.0有所回落，但该报告仍表明制造业基础依然稳固。继经历了10个月的收缩期后，6月该指数已连续第六个月保持在50.0的扩张临界点之上。该读数也与美国整体经济的持续增长相一致，ISM指出，历史上PMI高于47.5通常预示着整体经济扩张。深入分析数据细节可发现，继5月份强劲表现后，报告显示经济活动有所放缓。新订单指数从56.8降至56.0，生产指数从54.3降至52.2，表明需求和产出依然健康，但扩张步伐有所放缓。就业指数从48.6升至49.7，尽管仍低于50.0的临界点，表明制造业就业人数继续减少。新出口订单指数回落至48.5，再次进入收缩区间，凸显海外需求疲软。该报告中较为令人鼓舞的一方面是通胀状况。 “支付价格”指数从82.1大幅回落至73.0，远低于78.0的共识预期，表明当月投入成本压力显著缓解。与此同时，供应商交货速度放缓的幅度较5月有所收窄，表明供应链状况持续改善；而由于制造商保持精简的库存水平，库存仍处于收缩状态。总体而言，6月份ISM报告表明，美国制造业仍处于扩张态势，但在5月份激增之后，增速已转为更为稳健的节奏。新订单和产量的持续增长、价格压力的缓解以及就业状况的轻微改善，这些因素综合来看应被视为积极信号。尽管出口需求放缓值得关注，但该报告仍与经济持续以温和速度增长且未出现过热迹象的态势相符。5月份美国建筑支出基本持平，在4月份数据上修后，环比上升0.1%，经季节性调整后的年化率为2.210万亿美元，但仍比去年同期低1.5%。 2026年前五个月，建筑支出总额为8584亿美元，较2025年同期下降2.7%，凸显了该行业增长放缓的趋势。私营建筑业基本持平，住宅支出增长0.3%，但被非住宅项目支出下降0.3%所抵消。公共建筑业表现最为亮眼，环比增长0.5%，其中教育和公路建设均增长0.6%，成为主要驱动力。总体而言，该报告表明建筑业在月度层面趋于稳定，但与一年前相比仍面临逆风。美元当日收高。美元兑主要货币普遍走强，在交易最活跃的七种货币中，有六种货币兑美元汇率上涨。唯一的例外是英镑，其兑美元汇率小幅走高。在波动最大的货币中，澳元下跌0.36%至0.6894，而欧元表现最弱，下跌0.38%至1.1378。新西兰元下跌0.11%至0.5670。瑞士法郎和加元分别下跌0.15%和0.13%。 美元兑日元小幅上涨0.01%，报162.56，当日净变动幅度微乎其微。不过，美元兑日元在美股交易时段回落前，曾一度触及162.83的40年新高。唯一表现优于美元的货币是英镑，英镑兑美元上涨0.13%至1.3277，使英镑成为对美元表现最强劲的主要货币。总体而言，美元指数（DXY）上涨0.20%至101.388，凸显了美元在收盘前展现出的全面强势。美国股市今日收跌，道指基本持平，标普500指数下跌0.22%，纳斯达克指数下跌0.66%。Meta今日早些时候宣布将寻求出售其过剩的人工智能算力，这提振了Meta（这是否属于新的业务流），但打压了部分芯片及AI基础设施公司。对于一个建立在稀缺性而非过剩基础上的市场而言，“过剩供应”一词令人担忧。Meta股价上涨8.81%，美光（Micron）下跌10.57%，目前已跌破上周财报发布前的收盘价1048.50美元。收盘价为1032.28美元。英特尔（Intel）下跌9.03%。 美国股市中，AMD下跌6.29%，SanDisk下跌10.62%，博通下跌2.23%，Nebius下跌17.01%。该市场收益率走高，收益率曲线趋陡：2年期收益率4.178%，上涨3.9个基点；5年期收益率4.238%，上涨5.08个基点。10年期收益率为4.481%，上涨6.0个基点；30年期收益率为4.975%， 上涨7.2个基点。其他市场方面：原油收盘走低，价格下跌1.41%，报68.09美元；黄金上涨28美元（涨幅0.69%），报4035美元；白银上涨0.53美元（涨幅0.97%），报59.07美元；比特币交易价格略低于60,000美元，报59938美元。本文作者为investinglive.com的格雷格·米哈洛夫斯基。

Among the biggest movers, the Australian dollar fell 0.36% to 0.6894, while the euro was the weakest, declining 0.38% to 1.1378. The New Zealand dollar slipped 0.11% to 0.5670. The CHF and the CAD fell by 0.15% and 0.13% respectively. Against the Japanese yen, USDJPY edged up 0.01% to 162.56, reflecting little net change on the day. However, the USDJPY did reach a new 40 year high of 162.83 before moving lower in the US session The only currency to outperform the dollar was the British pound, with GBPUSD rising 0.13% to 1.3277, making sterling the strongest major currency versus the greenback. Overall, the U.S. Dollar Index (DXY) gained 0.20% to 101.388, underscoring the dollar's broad-based strength into the close. US stocks are ending the day down with the Dow near unchanged, the S&P down -0.22% and the Nasdaq falling -0.66%. Meta announced early in the day that they would look to sell their excess AI capacity which helped Meta (is it a new business flow) and hurt some of the chip. AI infrastructure companies. The word excess supply is scary to a market that is being built on scarcity, not excess. Meta rose 8.81% Micron fell -10.57% and is now below the closing level from before the earnings release last week at $1048.50. The price close that $1032.28. Intel fell -9.03%. AMD fell -6.29% Sandisk fell -10.62% Broadcom fell 2.23% Nebius fell -17.01% in the US that market, yields moved higher with a steeper yield curve: 2 year yield 4.178%, +3.9 basis points. 5 year yield 4.238%, +5.08 basis points. 10 year yield 4.481%, +6.0 basis points 30 year yield 4.975%, +7.2 basis points IN other markets: Crude oil is closing lower with the price trading down -1.41% at $68.09 Gold is trading up $28 or 0.69% at $4035 Silver is trading up $0.53 or 0.97% at $59.07 Bitcoin is trading just below $60,000 at $59938. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.impactZh: 央行政策预期变化可能提升主要货币对波动，杠杆仓位的保证金与回撤压力上升。
items.impactEn: Shifts in central-bank expectations may increase major-pair volatility and raise margin/drawdown pressure on leveraged positions.

items.title: ECB Forum: Lagarde had perfect monetary policy outlook to raise rates.
items.summary: ECB's Lagerde: Had perfect monetary policy Outlook to raise rates if I have one regret is is that I was bound by for guidance in the past. Looked at inflation outlook with risks. Risks are more broadly balanced than a few weeks ago. We are not in a stagflation environment We are not going to let inflation genie to get out of the bottle. Legarde on tasks forces says it is complicated. Fed's Warsh We are going to chart a new course so we can make better decisions There is a lot of data. I want to have a good family fight at the next meeting. Not going to give forward guidance. It is up to the central bank to decide if AI's inflationary AI below it showing itself first and very prominently in the US Exciting, consequential time to be a central banker. The US is likely to be a big winner with regard to AI. It is not a zero sum game. We are rooting for all countries to grow. We are in the first or second inning of this revolution I think that jobs will greater and prosperity will be stronger. We have a dual mandate and we after deliver on both the employment side and the price side Labor markets are steady. Supply side is steady. We are in a prices stability business We have looked around and see that prices are too high. Expectations of inflation have come down. Inflation risks have come down. We are going to deliver price stability in the US with the objective of 2% We will be an independent central bank. The most important thing we can do is get policy rate If communication is an obstacle, we should get rid of it. Volatility is down, yields are down. This is a rare moment to go back to first principles. We are creating task forces to get back to first principles. Task force leaders will be announced next week. We will get the best minds from inside and outside the US. Potential growth looks like it has trended. If last four quarters are and indication, there is reason to be optimistic Interest rates are the dominant means for which we should be making policy decisions Have not changed view on balance sheet in the first four weeks at the Fed. Want interest rate policy to be the key policy tool. BOE Gov Bailey: We've got a softening economy, labor market Decision not to raise rates was based on softening economy. Cutting rates is off the table at the moment We will return to enjoy Energy prices have come down. We have a delayed reaction mechanism to energy prices. The jury is still out with regard to AI and employment. We need stronger correlation to tackle risks stemming from AI models. Forward guidance can become quite problematic every time Anything we say can be interpreted as forward guidance. Is there something in these markets that can cause disruptions in the market. Is there tail risks? BOC Macklem We left policy rate unchanged, so yes we are comfortable where we are In economy, the economy is soft, but inflation is clearly above target. When we looked at the policy decision we think we are about at the right level to keep inflation contained. We have got to be humble amid uncertainty. If the situation changes we are prepared to take action. We are seeing the effects of US tariffs on exports. We see the tail and from the US, but also get the competitive pressures from the US. From financial stability point of view, stock valuations look stretched. Rise of hedge funds in the sovereign debt market makes you nervous that repo market disruptions could trigger rapid unwind. Market impact: 9:18 AM. The USD is moving higher with the GBPUSD testing the 100 hour moving average at 1.32224 and 200 hour moving average at 1.32145. The price will reached 1.3220. The EURUSD trades below the lows for the week near 1.1377 and further away from the 100 hour MA at 1.1395 9:21 AM ET: premarket for US stocks are showing S&P -12 points. Dow -6.2 points. NASDAQ -200 points 9:31 AM ET: Stock markets are open. A snapshot show: 9:39 AM ET: Nvidia -2.96% Google +1.0% micron -6.84% Meta +8.22% Microsoft +1.43% Apple +0.90% Amazon unchanged AMD -4.8% Broadcom -1.95% Marvell -4.64% Sandisk -8.22% 9:57 AM ET; USD reverses its course. GBPUSD trades to a new session by after bouncing off of the 100/200 hour moving averages. EURUSD extends back above its 100 and 200 hour moving averages at 1.1595 and 1.1400. USDJPY falls to a low of 162.30. The 100 hour moving average is still below at 162.058. Nevertheless, the price is moving away from the all-time high of 162.833. Quick summary; Forward guidance is on the back burner and a bad word. Less is more. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 欧洲央行拉加德：货币政策堪称完美。若要说有什么遗憾，那就是过去我受前瞻性指引的束缚。我们审视了通胀前景及其风险。与几周前相比，当前风险总体上更为均衡。我们并不处于滞胀环境中，也不会让通胀这只“魔鬼”从瓶中逃出。关于特别工作组，拉加德表示情况很复杂。美联储的沃什：我们将制定新的路线图，以便做出更明智的决策。数据量很大。我希望在下次会议上能进行一场“家庭争论”。不会提供前瞻性指引。人工智能是否会引发通胀，以及通胀是否会率先且显著地在美国显现，这取决于央行的判断。对于央行行长而言，这是一个激动人心且影响深远的时期。在人工智能领域，美国很可能成为最大赢家。这并非零和游戏。我们期待所有国家都能实现增长。我们正处于这场革命的第一或第二局。我认为就业机会将更加丰富，繁荣程度也将更上一层楼。我们肩负双重使命，且在就业和物价两个方面都已取得成效。劳动力市场保持稳定，供给侧也保持稳定。 我们的职责是维护物价稳定。我们观察到，当前物价水平过高。通胀预期已有所回落，通胀风险也已降低。我们将以2%为目标，在美國实现物价稳定。我们将保持作为独立央行的地位。我们最重要的是调整政策利率。如果沟通存在障碍，我们就应消除它。市场波动性下降，收益率走低。这是回归基本原则的难得时机。我们正在组建特别工作组以回归基本原则。工作组负责人将于下周公布。我们将汇聚来自美国国内外最优秀的人才。潜在增长率似乎已呈现趋势。如果过去四个季度的情况具有参考意义，那么我们有理由保持乐观。利率是我们制定政策决策的主要手段。在美联储任职的前四周里，我对资产负债表的看法没有改变。希望利率政策成为关键的政策工具。英国央行行长贝利：经济和劳动力市场正在放缓。决定不加息是基于经济放缓的考量。 目前降息已不在考虑之列。我们将重拾信心，因为能源价格已经回落。我们对能源价格的反应存在滞后机制。关于人工智能与就业的关系，目前尚无定论。我们需要建立更强的关联性，以应对人工智能模型带来的风险。前瞻性指引每次都可能带来相当大的问题——我们说的任何话都可能被解读为前瞻性指引。这些市场中是否存在可能引发市场动荡的因素？是否存在尾部风险？加拿大央行行长麦克莱姆：我们维持政策利率不变，因此对当前政策立场感到满意。经济方面，经济表现疲软，但通胀显然高于目标水平。在审视政策决策时，我们认为当前利率水平大致处于能够遏制通胀的合理区间。在不确定性面前，我们必须保持谦逊。如果形势发生变化，我们已做好采取行动的准备。我们正目睹美国关税对出口的影响。我们不仅看到来自美国的尾部风险，同时也面临来自美国的竞争压力。从金融稳定的角度看，股市估值似乎已显过高。对冲基金在主权债务市场中的崛起令人担忧，担心回购市场的动荡可能会引发快速平仓。 市场动态：上午9:18。美元走高，英镑兑美元正测试100小时移动平均线（1.32224）和200小时移动平均线（1.32145）。价格将升至1.3220。欧元兑美元（EURUSD）交投于本周低点1.1377附近下方，与1.1395的100小时均线进一步拉大距离。美国东部时间上午9:21：美股盘前行情显示，标普500指数下跌12点，道琼斯指数下跌6.2点，纳斯达克指数下跌200点。美国东部时间上午9:31：股市开盘。盘面快照显示：美东时间上午9:39：英伟达-2.96%，谷歌+1.0%，美光-6.84%，Meta+8.22%，微软+1.43%，苹果+0.90%，亚马逊持平，AMD-4.8%，博通-1.95%，Marvell-4.64%，闪迪-8.22%。美东时间上午9:57； 美元走势逆转。英镑兑美元在触及100/200小时移动平均线后反弹，创下盘中新高。欧元兑美元回升至1.1595和1.1400的100小时及200小时移动平均线之上。美元兑日元跌至162.30的低点。100小时移动平均线仍位于162.058下方。尽管如此，价格正远离162.833的历史高点。简要总结：前瞻性指引已被搁置，甚至成了一个贬义词。少即是多。本文作者为investinglive.com的Greg Michalowski。

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: Tech sell off slams Kospi, Nikkei lower too
items.summary: More on this: Trump earned $1.4bn from crypto in 2026 as most token holders sit at a loss US payrolls preview: June NFP seen slowing sharply to 110k after May's surge Bank of Korea official says Bank has capacity to take steps on the weak won Australia's trade blows out to big deficit vs. surplus expected Foreigner investors pull record $137bn from Asia stocks as AI rally forces rebalancing PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8088 (vs. estimate at 6.7929) Equites getting hit. South Korea Kospi dumped 6%. Japan's Nikkei down more than 2.5% JPMorgan sees dot-com era warning as AI hardware stocks diverge from spenders Korea inflation hits 2.5-year high as Goldman flags chip stock outflow risk UBS stays bullish on stocks after best quarter in six years, sees rally broadening Apple talks to buy memory chips from Pentagon blacklisted Chinese suppliers. Cook cooked? New Zealand building permits fall in May, down 4% m/m (prior +10.9) Recap: US, Iran end Doha talks with no breakthrough as Hormuz status stays unclear Equity market bargains grow scarce as AI boom lifts stocks to fresh highs MUFG sees euro rallying to 1.20 against dollar by 2027 as ECB nears rate peak Warsh, Lagarde and Bailey signal joint retreat from forward guidance at Sintra Preview: RBNZ tipped to hike 25bp in July as oil slide clouds tightening outlook investingLive Americas FX news wrap 1 Jul: Central bankers worry about inflation US stocks close lower. Midday rise in the Nasdaq fizzles Session Wrap Asia-Pac equities sold off heavily, led by a violent tech rotation out of AI-linked names after Meta's sudden capex discipline rattled Wall Street overnight South Korea bore the brunt, with the KOSPI slumping as much as 6% and triggering a sidecar as Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix each shed more than 7% Japan's Nikkei fell around 1%, softer than the regional selloff, while Hong Kong bucked the trend on local tech and biopharma strength as Shanghai stayed in the red Oil extended its grind lower as US-Iran talks in Doha wrapped with Qatar citing positive progress, though no breakthrough Gold pushed back above $4,000/oz, supported by softer jobs data and falling oil, with all eyes now on today's payrolls print for Fed cues Iron ore briefly topped $100/ton after China's state-backed buyer moved to restrict some Fortescue port inventories, though AUD slipped Apple in talks to source memory chips from blacklisted Chinese suppliers CXMT and YMTC, while Nikkei reports the company is planning five new iPhone models across H2 2026 and H1 2027 Regional equities copped a heavy hit through the session as a sharp rotation out of AI infrastructure names spilled across Asia-Pac markets. The catalyst traced back to Wall Street, where Meta's abrupt signal of capex discipline, reportedly including plans to sell off computing power, reignited concerns about overbuilt AI capacity and triggered a violent selloff in US tech that carried straight through into Asian trade. South Korea felt it hardest. The KOSPI slumped as much as 6% and tripped a sidecar circuit breaker in early trade, with Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix both losing more than 7% and erasing billions in combined market value as the broad US semiconductor rout hit chipmakers directly. Japan's Nikkei was comparatively more contained, down around 1% and off its worst levels of the session, though still weighed by the same tech selling alongside recent upside in yields. Greater China was mixed, with the mainland conforming to the risk-off tone while Hong Kong bucked the trend, supported by strength in local tech, biopharmaceutical and auto names on its return from holiday closure. US equity futures were sitting mildly positive into the local close. Elsewhere, oil continued its slow drift lower following the latest round of indirect US-Iran talks in Doha. Qatar's Foreign Ministry said mediators had concluded separate meetings with both sides and noted positive progress on issues tied to the Islamabad MoU, though no lasting breakthrough was signalled. Gold caught a bid on the back of softer than expected (US) jobs data Wednesday and the retreat in oil, with spot pushing back above the $4,000/oz mark and touching its highest level since June 23 in the prior session before today's payrolls report takes over as the next major catalyst for Fed pricing. Worth flagging for the medium term: a recent OMFIF survey found central banks themselves see gold trading in a $5,000 to $6,000/oz range in twelve months. In commodities, iron ore futures briefly cleared $100 a ton after China's state-backed buyer signalled plans to restrict some Fortescue inventories held at mainland ports. The Aussie slipped back. On the corporate side, Apple is reportedly in active talks to source memory chips from ChangXin Memory Technologies and Yangtze Memory Technologies, both on the Pentagon's Section 1260H blacklist, with any supply earmarked primarily for the Chinese market and no agreement yet finalised. Separately, Nikkei reports Apple is planning a five model iPhone lineup split across H2 2026 and H1 2027, including a larger volume of folding handsets than previously expected. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 更多相关内容：2026年特朗普从加密货币中获利14亿美元，而大多数代币持有者仍处于亏损状态 美国非农就业数据前瞻： 5月就业数据激增后，6月非农就业人数预计将大幅放缓至11万 韩国央行官员表示，央行有能力针对韩元走弱采取措施 澳大利亚贸易收支出现巨额赤字，与预期盈余形成反差 受AI行情推动，外国投资者从亚洲股市撤资创纪录的1370亿美元，迫使投资组合再平衡 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8088（市场预期为6.7929） 股市承压。韩国KOSPI指数暴跌6%。日本日经指数下跌逾2.5%。摩根大通认为AI硬件股与消费类股走势分化，预示着“互联网泡沫时代”的警钟。韩国通胀率创2.5年来新高，高盛警示芯片股资金流出风险。瑞银在创下六年最佳季度业绩后仍看多股市，预计涨势将进一步扩大。苹果正商谈从五角大楼黑名单上的中国供应商处采购内存芯片。 “Cook”出局？新西兰5月建筑许可量环比下降4%（前值+10.9） 回顾： 美国与伊朗多哈谈判无果而终，霍尔木兹海峡局势仍不明朗 随着AI热潮推动股市创下新高，股市中的超值股日益稀缺 三菱日联金融集团（MUFG）预计，随着欧洲央行利率接近峰值，到2027年欧元兑美元将升至1.20 沃什、拉加德和贝利在辛特拉会议上暗示将共同放弃前瞻性指引 预览： 市场预计新西兰央行将于7月加息25个基点，但油价下跌令紧缩前景蒙上阴影 investingLive美洲外汇新闻综述 7月1日：央行行长们担忧通胀，美股收低。 纳斯达克午盘涨势戛然而止 盘后综述 亚太股市大幅下挫，主要受科技股剧烈轮动拖累——此前Meta突然收紧资本支出计划，令隔夜华尔街市场震动。韩国股市受冲击最重，KOSPI指数一度暴跌6%，触发熔断机制，其中三星电子和SK海力士股价均下跌逾7% 日本日经指数下跌约1%，跌幅小于该地区整体抛售幅度；香港市场则逆势上扬，得益于本地科技股和生物制药股的强劲表现，而上海股市仍处于下跌状态。原油价格延续跌势，尽管卡塔尔表示多哈举行的美伊谈判取得了积极进展，但未取得突破。黄金价格重新回升至每盎司4,000美元以上， 这得益于疲软的就业数据和油价下跌，市场目光现正聚焦于今日的非农就业报告以寻找美联储政策信号；受中国国有买家采取措施限制部分Fortescue港口库存影响，铁矿石价格一度突破每吨100美元， 但澳元汇率仍出现下跌。苹果公司正与被列入黑名单的中国供应商CXMT和YMTC商谈采购内存芯片事宜，而《日经新闻》报道称，该公司计划在2026年下半年至2027年上半年推出五款新款iPhone。受AI基础设施板块资金大幅撤出的影响，区域股市在整个交易时段内遭受重创 亚太市场股指全线下跌。这一走势的导火索源自华尔街：据报道，Meta突然释放出将严格控制资本支出的信号，其中包括出售计算能力的计划，这重新点燃了市场对人工智能产能过剩的担忧，并引发了美国科技股的剧烈抛售，这一跌势直接蔓延至亚洲交易时段。韩国市场受冲击最为严重。韩国KOSPI指数早盘一度暴跌6%，触发“侧车”熔断机制；受美国半导体板块全面暴跌的直接冲击，三星电子和SK海力士双双下跌逾7%，合计蒸发了数十亿市值。相比之下，日本日经指数跌幅相对有限，下跌约1%，且已从盘中低点回升，但仍受科技股抛售及近期收益率上升的双重拖累。大中华区股市涨跌不一：内地股市顺应了避险情绪，而香港股市则逆势上扬，得益于假期休市后本地科技、生物制药和汽车类股的强劲表现。美国股指期货在当地收盘前小幅走高。此外，在多哈举行新一轮美伊间接谈判后，油价继续缓慢走低。 卡塔尔外交部表示，调解员已分别与双方举行了会谈，并指出在与《伊斯兰堡谅解备忘录》相关的问题上取得了积极进展，尽管尚未出现持久的突破。受周三（美国）就业数据弱于预期以及油价回落的提振，黄金买盘涌现，现货金价重新回升至每盎司4,000美元上方，并在今日非农就业报告发布前一交易日触及6月23日以来的最高水平——该报告将成为美联储政策定价的下一个主要催化剂。值得关注的中期因素：OMFIF最近的一项调查显示，各国央行自身预计未来12个月黄金价格将在每盎司5,000至6,000美元区间内波动。在大宗商品方面，中国国有买家暗示计划限制福特斯克（Fortescue）在内地港口的部分库存后，铁矿石期货价格一度突破每吨100美元。澳元汇率随之回落。企业方面，据报道，苹果公司正在积极与长信存储和长江存储进行谈判，计划从这两家均被列入五角大楼第1260H条黑名单的企业采购内存芯片，相关供应主要面向中国市场，但目前尚未达成最终协议。 此外，《日经新闻》报道称，苹果计划推出五款iPhone机型，发布时间将分在2026年下半年和2027年上半年，其中折叠屏手机的产量将高于此前预期。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。
items.summaryEn: More on this: Trump earned $1.4bn from crypto in 2026 as most token holders sit at a loss US payrolls preview: June NFP seen slowing sharply to 110k after May's surge Bank of Korea official says Bank has capacity to take steps on the weak won Australia's trade blows out to big deficit vs. surplus expected Foreigner investors pull record $137bn from Asia stocks as AI rally forces rebalancing PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8088 (vs. estimate at 6.7929) Equites getting hit. South Korea Kospi dumped 6%. Japan's Nikkei down more than 2.5% JPMorgan sees dot-com era warning as AI hardware stocks diverge from spenders Korea inflation hits 2.5-year high as Goldman flags chip stock outflow risk UBS stays bullish on stocks after best quarter in six years, sees rally broadening Apple talks to buy memory chips from Pentagon blacklisted Chinese suppliers. Cook cooked? New Zealand building permits fall in May, down 4% m/m (prior +10.9) Recap: US, Iran end Doha talks with no breakthrough as Hormuz status stays unclear Equity market bargains grow scarce as AI boom lifts stocks to fresh highs MUFG sees euro rallying to 1.20 against dollar by 2027 as ECB nears rate peak Warsh, Lagarde and Bailey signal joint retreat from forward guidance at Sintra Preview: RBNZ tipped to hike 25bp in July as oil slide clouds tightening outlook investingLive Americas FX news wrap 1 Jul: Central bankers worry about inflation US stocks close lower. Midday rise in the Nasdaq fizzles Session Wrap Asia-Pac equities sold off heavily, led by a violent tech rotation out of AI-linked names after Meta's sudden capex discipline rattled Wall Street overnight South Korea bore the brunt, with the KOSPI slumping as much as 6% and triggering a sidecar as Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix each shed more than 7% Japan's Nikkei fell around 1%, softer than the regional selloff, while Hong Kong bucked the trend on local tech and biopharma strength as Shanghai stayed in the red Oil extended its grind lower as US-Iran talks in Doha wrapped with Qatar citing positive progress, though no breakthrough Gold pushed back above $4,000/oz, supported by softer jobs data and falling oil, with all eyes now on today's payrolls print for Fed cues Iron ore briefly topped $100/ton after China's state-backed buyer moved to restrict some Fortescue port inventories, though AUD slipped Apple in talks to source memory chips from blacklisted Chinese suppliers CXMT and YMTC, while Nikkei reports the company is planning five new iPhone models across H2 2026 and H1 2027 Regional equities copped a heavy hit through the session as a sharp rotation out of AI infrastructure names spilled across Asia-Pac markets. The catalyst traced back to Wall Street, where Meta's abrupt signal of capex discipline, reportedly including plans to sell off computing power, reignited concerns about overbuilt AI capacity and triggered a violent selloff in US tech that carried straight through into Asian trade. South Korea felt it hardest. The KOSPI slumped as much as 6% and tripped a sidecar circuit breaker in early trade, with Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix both losing more than 7% and erasing billions in combined market value as the broad US semiconductor rout hit chipmakers directly. Japan's Nikkei was comparatively more contained, down around 1% and off its worst levels of the session, though still weighed by the same tech selling alongside recent upside in yields. Greater China was mixed, with the mainland conforming to the risk-off tone while Hong Kong bucked the trend, supported by strength in local tech, biopharmaceutical and auto names on its return from holiday closure. US equity futures were sitting mildly positive into the local close. Elsewhere, oil continued its slow drift lower following the latest round of indirect US-Iran talks in Doha. Qatar's Foreign Ministry said mediators had concluded separate meetings with both sides and noted positive progress on issues tied to the Islamabad MoU, though no lasting breakthrough was signalled. Gold caught a bid on the back of softer than expected (US) jobs data Wednesday and the retreat in oil, with spot pushing back above the $4,000/oz mark and touching its highest level since June 23 in the prior session before today's payrolls report takes over as the next major catalyst for Fed pricing. Worth flagging for the medium term: a recent OMFIF survey found central banks themselves see gold trading in a $5,000 to $6,000/oz range in twelve months. In commodities, iron ore futures briefly cleared $100 a ton after China's state-backed buyer signalled plans to restrict some Fortescue inventories held at mainland ports. The Aussie slipped back. On the corporate side, Apple is reportedly in active talks to source memory chips from ChangXin Memory Technologies and Yangtze Memory Technologies, both on the Pentagon's Section 1260H blacklist, with any supply earmarked primarily for the Chinese market and no agreement yet finalised. Separately, Nikkei reports Apple is planning a five model iPhone lineup split across H2 2026 and H1 2027, including a larger volume of folding handsets than previously expected. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
